source: decider run 2026-04-18T14:21Z — EXP-184 curmudgeon review holes (critical/major/moderate)

patches.rationale: CRITICAL: part8b uses 'Kill-Shot #1' to label the Section 4.1 independence audit. The actual Kill-Shot #1 in Part 5 is the Sydney-Perth distance test. This mislabel is a cheap rhetorical target for any dome defender reading the section.
CRITICAL: second 'Kill-Shot #1' mislabel in 8b.2(d) closer — same issue, remove incorrect label.

patches.find: Part 4.2 (Registry Framing Audit — the general API-vs-human-text pattern, pending integration via EXP-183); Kill-Shot #1 (the independence audit of the scored-domains dashboard).
patches.replace: the Registry Framing Audit (Part 4.2 — the general API-vs-human-text pattern); Section 4.1 (the independence audit of the scored-domains dashboard).
patches.rationale: CRITICAL: third 'Kill-Shot #1' mislabel in 8b.7, plus stale 'pending integration via EXP-183' (EXP-183 is already integrated). Fixed both in one patch. Also puts the title before the section number to disambiguate from the Eclipse 4.2.

patches.find: Part 4.2 (Registry Framing Audit, pending integration via EXP-183). Cross-references
patches.rationale: MAJOR: 'pending integration via EXP-183' is stale — EXP-183 is live. Also puts title before number to disambiguate from Eclipse Part 4.2.

patches.find: L3 (PRED-095, PRED-TIER-2): PRED-095 is genuinely prospective (registered 2026-04-04, window closes 2026-08-12, verdict pending); PRED-TIER-2 is recycled (restates WIN-011 with 1997 data). Under adoption we publish an explicit Tier 3 pass/fail threshold — "Pass = observed −17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage stations, ≥3 independent sites, pre-registered instruments; Fail = outside this range, or signal explained by ionospheric SQ variation alone." If the dome has not pinned the threshold to this precision, we cite the gap as our finding.
patches.replace: L3a (PRED-095, magnetic): Genuinely prospective (registered 2026-04-04, window closes 2026-08-12, verdict pending). Under adoption we publish an explicit magnetic threshold — "Pass = observed −17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage stations, ≥3 independent sites, pre-registered instruments; Fail = outside this range, or signal explained by ionospheric SQ variation alone." If the dome has not pinned the threshold to this precision, we cite the gap as our finding. L3b (PRED-TIER-2, gravity): Recycled — restates WIN-011 with 1997 Wang et al. gravity-eclipse data; the falsification criterion for this layer is independent replication of the gravity signal at the 2026 eclipse sites.
patches.rationale: MAJOR: L3 bundles PRED-095 (magnetic, −17 to −21 nT threshold) with PRED-TIER-2 (gravity, recycled WIN-011). The magnetic threshold was being implicitly applied to a gravity prediction. Split into L3a (magnetic) and L3b (gravity) with appropriate criteria for each.

patches.replace: (L3a) Tier 3 magnetic falsifier — PRED-095 (August 2026 eclipse); (L3b) Tier 3 gravity — PRED-TIER-2 (recycled WIN-011)
patches.rationale: MAJOR: Update the summary line to match the L3a/L3b split in the detailed text.

patches.find: Our WIN-058 and WIN-058b already reflect pending / not-demonstrated
patches.replace: Our WIN-058 already reflects Misleading and WIN-058b reflects Not Demonstrated
patches.rationale: MODERATE: 8b.4 TLDR says WIN-058 reflects 'pending' but WIN-058 verdict is actually 'Misleading'. The body paragraph correctly states WIN-058 as Misleading; TLDR was inconsistent.

patches.find: landed in the same multi-file push window as
patches.replace: landed in the same 24-hour detection window as
patches.rationale: MODERATE: 'multi-file push window' conflates a git push operation with a detection time window. Three separate commits (6e06efb, 5021eec, a57e731) are named; these may have been in one push or multiple. 'Detection window' is the correct term for our monitoring frame.
